theory.canonical_statement: ID 423 establishes the electrical drive protocol for the 12 node transducer array embedded within the SS304L torus. These nodes function as a phased array system that generates a rotating electromagnetic "well." By utilizing the Pi Model synchronization logic, the array does not merely pull the rotor toward a single point; it creates two symmetrical stable equilibrium points separated by 180. This bi stable configuration is the "Flip State" synchronization required for high fault tolerance. If the system encounters an external perturbationsuch as a sudden change in vacuum densitythe 12 node array allows the ferrite core to "slip" by exactly radians without losing frequency lock. This ensures that the up refresh rate [ID 407] remains continuous, preventing the "Causal Phase Slip" that would otherwise result in energy decoherence.
